slides.heading: Meet Priya — building her first todo app
slides.body:
Priya is learning to code. She's built a simple todo app — it works, and she's proud of it.

Then she decides to add a new feature: drag-and-drop tasks. She starts rewriting parts of her code.

Two hours later? Nothing works. The drag-and-drop is broken. And so is the part that was working fine before.

Can she go back? No. She saved over everything.
bullets: 💻 Her todo app was working beautifully
✋ She changed the code to add a new feature
😭 No way back. The working version is gone.

slides.heading: Problem 2 — the folder of doom
slides.body:
Priya is smart. After that disaster, she starts making backup copies before changing anything.

A week later, her project folder looks like this:

todo-app/
todo-backup-jan5.zip
todo-WORKING-VERSION.zip
todo-before-dragdrop.zip
todo-final-v2.zip
todo-USE_THIS_ONE.zip

She has 6 versions. She has no idea which one is the real one. Which had the bug fix? Which had the dark mode?
bullets: 🗑️ Afraid to delete any in case she needs it
🕐 Wasted time digging through old zips
🧠 She needs to remember every version in her head

slides.heading: Problem 3 — working with a friend
slides.body:
Priya's friend Raj wants to help. Great!

They both download the latest version. Priya works on the login page. Raj works on the settings page.

Raj finishes first and sends her the new file. Priya copies it in. Her login page changes — GONE. His file overwrote everything.

How do two people work on the same project without destroying each other's work?
bullets: 💥 One person's work erases the other's

slides.heading: Git solves ALL three problems
slides.body:
Git was invented specifically for these problems. It's what every developer in the world uses.

Tap each problem to see how Git solves it.
bullets: Problem 1: Can't go back → Git saves checkpoints (commits) you can always return to
Problem 2: Folder of doom → Git stores all versions with labels, automatically
Problem 3: Two people overwriting → Git tracks every change by every person and merges them safely
